meta.framework: ANZSCO + NZ Construction Industry Council (CIVENZ / CCNZ)
meta.safety_act: Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (HSWA)

duties: Carry out structural timber framing, formwork, and rough carpentry works on residential and commercial construction sites in accordance with project plans and NZBC requirements.
Install, fit, and fix wooden structures including wall frames, roof trusses, flooring systems, and subfloor frameworks as per engineering specifications.
Install interior and exterior joinery including doors, windows, skirting boards, architraves, and built-in cabinetry to specified tolerances.
Read, interpret, and work from architectural drawings, construction plans, and technical specifications to ensure accurate execution of carpentry tasks.
Measure, mark, cut, and shape timber and timber-based materials using hand tools and power tools including saws, routers, nail guns, and pneumatic equipment.
Ensure all carpentry works comply with the New Zealand Building Code (NZBC), relevant New Zealand Standards (NZS), and site-specific safety requirements.
Erect and dismantle temporary structures, formwork, scaffolding supports, and hoarding as required during construction phases.
Carry out weatherboard cladding, fascia, soffit, and exterior finishing works to manufacturer specifications and weathertightness standards.
Inspect completed works for quality assurance, identify defects, and carry out remedial or rectification works as directed.
Complete all required site documentation including timesheets, work orders, and compliance checklists, and report progress to the Site Supervisor.
Maintain all hand tools, power tools, and equipment in a clean, safe, and operational condition and report faults promptly.
Adhere to all company Health and Safety policies, HSWA 2015 obligations, and site safety plans including use of appropriate PPE at all times.
Erect, assemble, and connect structural steel members including columns, beams, trusses, purlins, and bracing systems for commercial, industrial, and infrastructure projects.
Read and interpret structural steel drawings, connection details, and erection sequences to plan and execute steelwork in accordance with engineer specifications.
Operate cranes, hoists, and rigging equipment to lift and position steel members safely, in accordance with NZ crane operator licensing requirements.
Install and tighten high-strength structural bolts, set bearing plates, and grout column base plates to specified torque values and tolerances.
Carry out field welding of structural connections using MIG, MMA, or flux-cored arc welding as required by the structural design.
Ensure all structural steel erection complies with NZS 3404 (Steel Structures Standard) and structural engineer specifications.
Erect and use temporary bracing, guys, and support systems to maintain stability of the structure during erection.
Carry out plumbing, levelling, and alignment of steel members using theodolites, levels, and measuring equipment.
Operate elevated work platforms, man cages, and fall arrest systems safely when working at height.
Inspect erected steelwork for compliance with drawings and specifications, and report non-conformances to the Structural Engineer.
Maintain all erection tools, equipment, and rigging gear in a safe and serviceable condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and working at heights regulations at all times.
Cut, bend, shape, and place reinforcing steel bars (rebar) and mesh in accordance with structural drawings, bar bending schedules, and engineer specifications.
Assemble and tie rebar cages, mats, and frames for footings, slabs, columns, beams, walls, and bridge decks using approved tie wire and bar chairs.
Install and fix post-tensioning and pre-tensioning systems including duct placement, anchorage installation, and sheathing as per post-tensioning drawings.
Operate rebar bending machines, cutting machines, and mechanical splicing equipment safely and in accordance with manufacturer guidelines.
Position and secure reinforcement to specified cover dimensions using bar chairs, spacers, and supports to ensure correct concrete cover.
Read and interpret structural drawings, reinforcement layout plans, and bar bending schedules to plan and execute reinforcing works accurately.
Carry out inspection of placed reinforcement and prepare for engineer hold point inspections prior to concrete placement.
Ensure all reinforcing works comply with NZS 3101 (Concrete Structures), project quality plans, and structural engineer requirements.
Erect and dismantle formwork, falsework, and support systems as required in conjunction with reinforcing placement.
Operate elevated work platforms, cranes, and lifting equipment in a safe and certified manner as required on site.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.
Complete all required site documentation, inspection records, and report deficiencies or non-conformances to the Site Supervisor promptly.
Design, fabricate, erect, and strip all types of concrete formwork systems including engineered panel systems, traditional timber formwork, and proprietary shutter systems.
Erect falsework, propping, and shoring systems for elevated slabs, suspended floors, bridges, and transfer structures to engineer-approved designs.
Read and interpret formwork design drawings, shoring plans, and structural engineer specifications to plan and execute formwork works safely.
Install waterstops, cast-in items, starter bars, sleeves, and penetrations within formwork prior to concrete placement.
Strip and clean formwork and falsework components after concrete has achieved specified stripping strength.
Carry out formwork inspections prior to concrete placement and participate in engineer hold point checks.
Ensure all formwork and falsework complies with AS/NZS 3610 (Formwork for Concrete) and the structural engineer design.
Operate cranes, forklifts, and elevated work platforms to handle and position formwork components safely.
Maintain formwork panels, props, and components in a clean and serviceable condition and report damaged components.
Carry out layout, survey, and control of formwork positions and levels using levels and theodolites.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and confined space procedures.
Complete all required formwork inspection records and documentation as required by the project quality plan.
Erect, align, and strip formwork for concrete slabs, footings, foundations, columns, beams, retaining walls, and other structural elements.
Place, compact, level, and finish concrete using hand screeds, power screeds, floats, trowels, and vibrators to achieve specified surface finishes.
Install reinforcing steel (rebar) and mesh, including cutting, bending, tying, and placing rebar cages per structural engineer specifications.
Carry out concrete cutting, coring, breaking, and demolition works using diamond saws, core drills, and jackhammers.
Apply surface treatments and finishes to concrete including broomed, exposed aggregate, polished, and spray-on textured finishes.
Install expansion joints, saw-cut joints, crack inducers, and waterstops in concrete slabs and structures.
Operate concrete pumps, agitator trucks, kibbles, and placing booms to accurately place concrete in formwork.
Read and interpret structural drawings, formwork drawings, and concrete specifications accurately.
Ensure all concrete works comply with NZS 3109 (Concrete Construction), NZS 3101, and project quality requirements.
Carry out concrete curing procedures including wet curing, curing compounds, and insulation blankets.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.
Complete all required site documentation and quality records for concrete placement works.
Lay, bond, and finish brick, block, stone, and masonry units to construct walls, piers, columns, and arches for structural and non-structural applications.
Mix, apply, and finish mortar and grout using appropriate mix designs for substrate type, environment, and structural requirements.
Install and align reinforcing steel, ties, anchors, and connectors within masonry systems per structural engineer specifications.
Construct cavity wall systems, veneer cladding, and fire-rated masonry walls including wall ties, DPC, and weep holes.
Carry out restoration and remediation works on heritage masonry buildings including tuckpointing, repointing, and unit replacement.
Install lintels, sills, copings, corbels, and other masonry elements within brickwork and blockwork construction.
Lay paving, paths, driveways, and hardscaping using brick, clay pavers, concrete pavers, and natural stone.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, masonry layout plans, and specifications accurately.
Ensure all masonry works comply with NZS 4210 (Masonry Construction), NZBC, and structural engineer specifications.
Erect and use scaffolding, trestles, and working platforms safely per HSWA 2015 working at heights requirements.
Maintain all masonry tools, mixers, and equipment in a clean, safe, and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements and participate in quality inspections at structural masonry hold points.
Cut, dress, shape, and install natural stone including granite, marble, limestone, sandstone, and bluestone for architectural, heritage, and decorative applications.
Fabricate stone cladding panels, copings, sills, lintels, columns, and decorative features using hand tools and stone cutting machinery.
Install stone cladding systems on building facades including dry-fix and mortar-fix systems with appropriate anchoring details.
Carry out restoration and conservation of heritage stonework including cleaning, repointing, and replacement of damaged stone.
Lay natural stone flooring, paving, and landscaping using appropriate bedding, jointing, and sealing systems.
Carve decorative stonework, inscriptions, memorials, and artistic features using hand and pneumatic tools.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, stone fabrication drawings, and heritage conservation specifications accurately.
Ensure all stonework complies with relevant New Zealand Standards and heritage conservation requirements.
Select appropriate stone materials, adhesives, mortars, and fixing systems for the application and environment.
Operate lifting equipment and vacuum lifters to handle and position large stone panels safely.
Maintain all stone cutting tools, polishing equipment, and machinery in safe and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE including dust extraction.
Operate heavy earthmoving plant including excavators, bulldozers, graders, scrapers, and compactors for bulk earthworks and site formation.
Carry out excavation for foundations, trenches, basements, retaining walls, roads, and underground services to specified dimensions.
Operate wheeled loaders, skid steer loaders, and telehandlers for material handling, stockpiling, and loading operations.
Carry out compaction of fill materials using smooth drum rollers, padfoot rollers, and plate compactors to specified density.
Operate tip trucks and water carts for material transport, watering, and dust suppression operations.
Carry out subgrade preparation, road base laying, and pavement construction for roads and hardstand areas.
Read and interpret civil engineering drawings, earthworks plans, and survey setout pegs to execute earthworks accurately.
Operate plant safely near underground services including identification, hand exposure, and appropriate safe working distances.
Carry out plant pre-start checks, daily maintenance, and defect reporting per manufacturer requirements.
Comply with Traffic Management Plans (TMP), site traffic rules, and Health and Safety at Work Act 2015.
Hold current operator certificates for all plant operated, including relevant WorkSafe NZ high-risk work licences.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and environmental controls at all times.
Install, maintain, and repair below-ground drainage systems including stormwater, wastewater, and subsoil drainage pipework.
Carry out excavation, trench preparation, pipe laying, bedding, backfilling, and compaction to specified grades and depths.
Install drainage pipes, fittings, and appurtenances including uPVC, HDPE, concrete, and ductile iron pipework.
Install manholes, inspection chambers, sumps, soakage systems, and stormwater detention tanks per specifications.
Connect new drainage systems to existing council reticulation networks in accordance with local authority requirements.
Carry out CCTV drainage inspections, drain tracing, and blockage clearing using jetting equipment and drain rods.
Perform pressure testing, air testing, and water testing of installed drainage systems prior to backfilling.
Ensure all drainlaying works are carried out under a current Licensed Drainlayer registration issued by the PGDB New Zealand.
Read and interpret civil engineering drawings, drainage layout plans, and council standard drawings accurately.
Liaise with local councils, building inspectors, and project engineers for drainage inspections and CCC.
Operate excavators, compactors, and laser levels safely per site traffic management and Health and Safety plans.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and confined space entry procedures.
Lay, compact, and finish asphalt and bituminous surfacing for roads, carparks, driveways, footpaths, and airfields to specified thickness and tolerances.
Operate paving machines, asphalt rakers, and compaction rollers to achieve smooth and uniform pavement surfaces.
Prepare subgrade and basecourse materials including trimming, compaction, and proof rolling prior to asphalt paving.
Apply prime coat, tack coat, and bituminous emulsions to prepared surfaces prior to asphalt placement.
Cut, trim, and repair existing asphalt surfaces including patching, overlay, and mill-and-fill operations.
Install kerb and channel, drainage channels, and road furniture in conjunction with pavement construction.
Carry out line marking, thermoplastic road markings, and pavement delineation as required.
Read and interpret civil engineering drawings, pavement design drawings, and road construction specifications accurately.
Ensure all pavement works comply with NZTA specifications, local authority requirements, and project quality plans.
Manage traffic control and site establishment in accordance with approved Traffic Management Plans (TMP).
Operate asphalt plant, paving equipment, and roller compactors safely per HSWA 2015 requirements.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE including heat-resistant footwear.
Drive, bore, and install piles including precast concrete, steel H-piles, CHS piles, bored cast-in-situ piles, and screw piles for foundations.
Operate pile driving rigs, drop hammers, hydraulic hammers, vibratory hammers, and diesel hammers to drive piles to specified set or refusal.
Carry out CFA, rotary bored, and driven cast-in-place pile installation using specialist drilling rigs.
Install ground anchors, soil nails, micropiles, and jet grout columns for retaining walls and slope stabilisation.
Carry out dynamic pile load testing and static pile load testing per engineer specifications.
Install sheet piles, king post walls, secant pile walls, and contiguous pile walls for excavation support.
Read and interpret geotechnical reports, pile design drawings, and installation specifications accurately.
Monitor pile installation records including set, driving logs, concrete volumes, and grout injection pressures.
Ensure all piling works comply with AS 2159 (Piling) and geotechnical engineer specifications.
Operate cranes, piling rigs, and supporting plant safely per NZ crane operator licensing requirements.
Carry out site preparation, temporary works, and dewatering associated with piling works.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and noise and vibration management plans.
Carry out civil construction works for bridges, culverts, retaining walls, seawalls, wharves, and other infrastructure structures.
Erect, align, and strip formwork for bridge decks, piers, abutments, and wing walls per structural engineer drawings.
Install precast bridge beams, deck units, and superstructure elements using cranes and specialist lifting equipment.
Install bridge bearings, expansion joints, drainage systems, and parapets to design specifications.
Carry out underwater works including cofferdam construction, pile installation, and substructure concrete in marine environments.
Install geosynthetic reinforcement, drainage layers, and select fill for reinforced earth retaining wall systems.
Carry out concrete placement, compaction, and finishing for structural bridge elements to specified tolerances.
Monitor structural movements, settlements, and displacements using survey instruments and monitoring equipment.
Carry out maintenance and rehabilitation works on existing bridges including bearing replacement and concrete repair.
Read and interpret structural drawings, erection sequences, and construction methodology plans accurately.
Ensure all works comply with NZTA Bridge Manual, relevant NZ Standards, and structural engineer specifications.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and maritime safety requirements.
Carry out underground excavation works including drill and blast, roadheader, and TBM-assisted tunnelling for road, rail, and utility tunnels.
Operate underground drilling rigs, jumbo drills, and explosives charging equipment for rock excavation per approved drill and blast designs.
Install ground support systems including rock bolts, mesh, shotcrete, and steel ribs to engineer-designed ground support patterns.
Apply shotcrete (sprayed concrete) to tunnel walls and crowns using wet or dry process spray equipment.
Carry out excavation mucking using underground loaders, haul trucks, and conveyor systems.
Install precast concrete segmental lining, grouting, and waterproofing systems for bored tunnels.
Operate shaft sinking equipment, stage winches, and kibble systems for vertical shaft construction.
Monitor tunnel deformation, convergence, and ground movement using survey instruments and automated monitoring systems.
Handle, store, and use explosives and detonators per the HSNO Act and site-specific blasting procedures.
Carry out gas monitoring, ventilation management, and emergency procedures in underground environments.
Ensure all underground works comply with WorkSafe NZ underground mining regulations and project safety management plans.
Comply with all underground Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE including self-rescuers.
Install, maintain, repair, and commission cold water, hot water, and sanitary drainage systems per NZS 3604 and NZBC requirements.
Lay, join, test, and commission water supply and drainage pipes using copper, CPVC, PEX, uPVC, and stainless steel materials.
Install and connect sanitary fixtures and fittings including toilets, basins, baths, showers, sinks, and urinals.
Install and commission hot water systems including electric, gas, solar, and heat pump water heaters.
Carry out pressure testing, leak detection, flow testing, and commissioning of all installed pipework and systems.
Install stormwater, roof drainage, and subsoil drainage systems including gutters, downpipes, and soakage systems.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, hydraulic services drawings, and plumbing specifications accurately.
Ensure all plumbing works comply with the Building Act 2004, NZBC G12 (Water Supplies), G13 (Foul Water), and NZ Standards.
Liaise with building inspectors, council officers, and certifying authorities for inspections and code compliance certificates.
Maintain a clean and safe work environment and adhere to all site Health and Safety requirements under HSWA 2015.
Complete all required compliance documentation, as-built drawings, producer statements, and maintenance manuals.
Perform routine maintenance, fault diagnosis, and emergency repairs on existing plumbing and drainage systems.
Install, commission, maintain, and repair natural gas and LPG reticulation systems and appliances per NZS 5261 and NZBC.
Install gas supply pipework including copper, stainless steel CSST, and PE pipework using approved jointing methods.
Install and commission gas appliances including cooktops, ovens, heaters, boilers, and hot water systems.
Carry out gas pressure testing, tightness testing, and commissioning of all installed gas systems prior to handover.
Identify and rectify gas leaks, appliance faults, and system deficiencies using appropriate gas detection equipment.
Install gas meter sets, regulators, emergency control valves, and automatic gas shutoff systems.
Ensure all gasfitting works are carried out under a current Licensed Gasfitter registration issued by the PGDB New Zealand.
Issue Certificates of Compliance (CoC) for all completed gasfitting works per the Gas Act 1992 and PGDB requirements.
Read and interpret gas installation drawings, appliance installation manuals, and project specifications accurately.
Liaise with gas network operators for connections, metering, and purging of new gas installations.
Maintain all gasfitting tools, test equipment, and personal protective equipment in safe and calibrated condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and gas safety emergency procedures at all times.
Install, commission, maintain, and repair HVAC systems including split systems, ducted systems, VRF/VRV, and chilled water systems.
Install refrigerant pipework, insulation, drainage pipework, and electrical connections per manufacturer specifications and AS/NZS 1677.
Commission and test HVAC systems including leak testing, evacuation, refrigerant charging, and airflow balancing.
Install ductwork systems including sheet metal ducts, flexible ducts, diffusers, grilles, dampers, and fire/smoke dampers.
Carry out fault diagnosis and repair of HVAC equipment including compressors, condensers, evaporators, fans, and pumps.
Install BMS sensors, controllers, and actuators and integrate HVAC equipment with building management systems.
Handle, recover, and recharge refrigerants per the Ozone Layer Protection Act 1996 using an approved refrigerant handling licence.
Carry out preventive maintenance on HVAC equipment including filter changes, coil cleaning, and calibration.
Read and interpret mechanical services drawings, refrigeration schematics, and HVAC specifications accurately.
Ensure all HVAC works comply with NZBC G4 (Ventilation), relevant NZ and Australian Standards, and project specifications.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and electrical safety requirements.
Complete all required commissioning records, maintenance logs, and compliance documentation upon completion.
Install, commission, test, and maintain fire sprinkler systems, fire hydrant systems, hose reel systems, and deluge systems per NZS 4541 and NZS 4510.
Install fire detection and alarm systems including smoke detectors, heat detectors, manual call points, and control panels per NZS 4512.
Install gaseous suppression systems including CO2, FM-200, and Novec 1230 for data centres and special hazard applications.
Carry out pressure testing, flow testing, and full functional testing of fire suppression and detection systems.
Install fire doors, fire dampers, fire stopping, and passive fire protection systems per NZBC C3 (Spread of Fire).
Carry out routine inspection, testing, and maintenance of fire protection systems per NZS 4541 maintenance requirements.
Install kitchen suppression systems, paint spray booth suppression, and industrial fire protection systems.
Read and interpret fire engineering drawings, hydraulic calculations, and system specifications accurately.
Liaise with building consent authorities, fire engineers, and NZ Fire and Emergency for system approvals and commissioning.
Issue certificates of compliance and system maintenance records for all installed and maintained fire protection systems.
Maintain all installation tools, test equipment, and gauges in a calibrated and serviceable condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.
Fabricate, install, modify, and maintain process pipework systems for industrial, petrochemical, food processing, and power generation facilities.
Read and interpret piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs), isometric drawings, and piping specifications accurately.
Cut, bevel, fit, and join process pipes using welding, flanging, threading, and mechanical coupling methods per pressure rating.
Install pipe supports, hangers, expansion loops, and restraints per pipe stress analysis and support spacing requirements.
Carry out hydrostatic pressure testing, pneumatic testing, and leak testing of completed pipework to specified test pressures.
Install valves, instrumentation, flow meters, safety relief valves, and process equipment within piping systems.
Apply thermal insulation, cladding, and heat tracing to process pipework for temperature control and personnel protection.
Carry out modifications, tie-ins, and hot tapping of existing live process systems using safe isolation and hot work procedures.
Ensure all process pipework complies with ASME B31.3 (Process Piping) or applicable NZ standards and P&ID requirements.
Participate in pre-commissioning, commissioning, and start-up activities for new process facilities.
Maintain all pipefitting tools, measuring equipment, and personal protective equipment in a safe and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, permit to work systems, and process safety requirements.
Install, test, commission, and maintain electrical wiring systems, switchboards, distribution boards, and associated equipment per AS/NZS 3000 and NZBC.
Install and connect electrical cables, conduits, trunking, cable trays, and containment systems for all installation types.
Install, wire, and commission lighting systems, power outlets, switches, circuit breakers, RCDs, and metering equipment.
Carry out installation and commissioning of three-phase and single-phase motor circuits, control panels, and industrial equipment.
Perform fault finding, diagnosis, and rectification on existing electrical installations using appropriate test instruments.
Install data cabling, communications wiring, fire alarm systems, security systems, and other low-voltage systems.
Carry out all electrical work under a current Electrical Worker Registration (EWR) issued by the EWRB New Zealand.
Ensure all electrical installations are inspected and certified by a Registered Electrical Inspector (REI) with Certificates of Compliance issued.
Read and interpret electrical drawings, single-line diagrams, schematics, and specifications accurately.
Liaise with the lines company, metering provider, and relevant authorities for connections and energisation.
Maintain all test equipment, tools, and personal protective equipment in safe and calibrated condition.
Complete all required compliance documentation including Certificates of Compliance, as-built records, and test results.
Install, test, commission, and maintain high voltage (HV) electrical systems including 11kV, 33kV, and 110kV switchgear, transformers, and substations.
Carry out HV cable jointing, terminating, and testing for underground and overhead HV distribution and transmission systems.
Install, test, and commission HV switchgear including ring main units (RMU), circuit breakers, disconnectors, and protection relays.
Carry out HV transformer installation, oil sampling, Buchholz relay testing, and tap changer maintenance.
Operate isolating and earthing equipment under live line working procedures and approved safety rules for HV systems.
Install and commission substation protection, control, and SCADA systems for HV network infrastructure.
Carry out HV cable fault location using time domain reflectometry (TDR) and fault burning equipment.
Carry out insulation resistance testing, partial discharge testing, and high voltage withstand testing of HV equipment.
Prepare and implement switching programs, safety isolations, and earthing schemes per utility safety rules.
Read and interpret HV engineering drawings, protection relay settings, and substation layout drawings accurately.
Ensure all HV works comply with Electricity (Safety) Regulations 2010, line owner safety rules, and AS/NZS 3000.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and electricity safety regulations for live line working.
Install, calibrate, test, commission, and maintain process instrumentation including pressure transmitters, flow meters, temperature sensors, and analysers.
Install field instruments and associated wiring, pneumatic tubing, impulse lines, and instrument supports in process plant environments.
Carry out loop checking, calibration verification, and functional testing of instrumentation and control systems.
Configure and program smart field instruments, PLCs, DCS systems, and SCADA systems using approved configuration tools.
Install and terminate instrument cables in marshalling cabinets, JBs, and control room panels per wiring schedules.
Carry out fault diagnosis and repair of failed instrumentation using calibration equipment, multimeters, and HART communicators.
Install and commission safety instrumented systems (SIS) and safety integrity level (SIL) rated instrumentation.
Carry out functional safety testing of SIS loops per IEC 61511 and the SIL verification documentation.
Read and interpret P&IDs, loop diagrams, wiring schedules, and instrument data sheets accurately.
Complete instrument index updates, calibration records, and as-built documentation for all installed instruments.
Ensure all instrumentation works comply with applicable hazardous area classification (ATEX/IECEx) requirements.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, permit to work systems, and process safety requirements.
Install, test, and commission grid-connected and off-grid solar photovoltaic (PV) systems on residential, commercial, and industrial buildings.
Install solar PV panels, mounting systems, racking, and roof penetrations per manufacturer specifications and AS/NZS 5033.
Install inverters, battery energy storage systems (BESS), switchboards, and metering equipment for solar PV installations.
Install and terminate DC and AC wiring for solar PV systems per AS/NZS 3000 and AS/NZS 5033.
Carry out installation of EV charging stations and associated switchboard modifications.
Carry out solar resource assessment, shading analysis, and system sizing for proposed solar PV installations.
Commission and test solar PV systems including insulation resistance testing, IV curve tracing, and performance verification.
Liaise with lines companies for G59 protection relay settings, export limiting, and grid connection approvals.
Register solar PV installations with the Electricity Authority and lines company per NZ connection requirements.
Complete all required Certificates of Compliance (CoC) and system commissioning documentation.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and working at heights regulations.
Maintain all installation tools, test equipment, and personal protective equipment in a safe and calibrated condition.
Install, terminate, test, and commission structured cabling systems including Cat 5e, Cat 6, Cat 6A, and fibre optic cabling.
Install telecommunications racks, cabinets, patch panels, outlets, and containment systems per TIA-568 and AS/NZS 3080.
Terminate and test copper data cabling using keystone jacks, patch panels, and cable testers.
Splice, terminate, and test fibre optic cables using fusion splicers, mechanical connectors, and OTDR test equipment.
Install and commission IP CCTV systems, access control systems, intercom systems, and audio-visual systems.
Install antenna systems, satellite dishes, and associated cabling for broadcast and communications applications.
Install and commission nurse call systems, PA/emergency warning systems, and building automation cabling.
Carry out cable management, labelling, and documentation of all installed cabling systems per project specifications.
Read and interpret building services drawings, cable schedules, and telecommunications specifications accurately.
Ensure all cabling installations comply with AS/NZS 3080 (Telecommunications Installations) and AS/NZS 3000.
Maintain all test equipment, fusion splicers, and cable installation tools in a calibrated and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.
Install, repair, and maintain metal roofing systems including corrugated iron, standing seam, concealed fix, and long-run roofing profiles to NZBC E2 requirements.
Install concrete and clay roof tiles, ridge cappings, hip cappings, and valley gutters per manufacturer installation guidelines.
Install roof flashings, aprons, soakers, step flashings, and penetration flashings to ensure weathertight junctions.
Fix and commission roof drainage systems including gutters, downpipes, sumps, overflows, and valley gutters per NZBC E1.
Carry out installation of roof windows, skylights, solar panel penetrations, and ventilation systems with weathertight detailing.
Perform roof inspections, defect identification, and remedial works including repointing, resealing, and tile replacement.
Install roof underlays, sarking, and insulation systems per NZBC H1 (Energy Efficiency) and manufacturer requirements.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, roofing specifications, and product data sheets accurately.
Erect and use edge protection, safety nets, roof anchors, and fall arrest systems per HSWA 2015 and WorkSafe NZ guidelines.
Ensure all roofing works comply with NZBC, relevant NZ Standards, and manufacturer CodeMark or Appraisal conditions.
Operate and maintain all roofing tools, bending machines, and power tools in a safe and operational condition.
Complete all required compliance documentation and participate in weathertightness inspections at key hold points.
Install exterior wall cladding systems including fibre cement, weatherboard, aluminium composite panels (ACP), and curtain wall systems.
Install cavity batten systems, rigid air barriers, and building wraps per NZBC E2 and manufacturer requirements.
Install Rockcote, Sto, and similar proprietary exterior insulation and finish systems (EIFS) per CodeMark conditions.
Cut, fix, and finish fibre cement sheet, Linea weatherboard, and similar products to specified profiles and fixing patterns.
Install aluminium composite panel (ACP) systems including subframe, cassette panels, and wet-sealed joint systems.
Install rainscreen cladding systems including terracotta, fibre cement, and metal rainscreen panels on commercial facades.
Flash and seal all cladding junctions, penetrations, windows, doors, and abutments to achieve weathertight performance.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, facade specifications, and cladding system installation manuals accurately.
Ensure all cladding works comply with NZBC E2 (External Moisture) and relevant CodeMark or Technical Appraisal conditions.
Carry out weathertightness testing including water testing, hose testing, and chamber testing of installed cladding systems.
Operate scaffolding, mast climbers, and elevated work platforms safely when working at height on facades.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.
Cut, shape, handle, and install glass panels, double-glazed units (IGUs), laminated glass, toughened glass, and specialty glass products.
Install aluminium window and door frames, curtain wall systems, shopfronts, structural glazing, and frameless glass systems.
Install and seal glazing units using appropriate setting blocks, glazing tapes, structural silicone, and weatherseal systems.
Carry out glass replacement, resealing, and remediation works on existing windows, doors, and glazed facades.
Install glass balustrades, frameless shower screens, glass splashbacks, and decorative glass features.
Carry out site measurement, template making, and survey for custom glazing fabrication orders.
Handle and transport large glass panels using suction lifters, A-frames, and mechanical handling equipment safely.
Ensure all glazing works comply with NZBC E2, NZBC H1, NZS 4223 (Glazing in Buildings), and Safety Glazing requirements.
Read and interpret architectural drawings, glazing schedules, and curtain wall drawings accurately.
Install framing, mullions, transoms, and support systems for glazing as required by the curtain wall or window system design.
Maintain all glazing tools, cutting equipment, and handling equipment in a safe and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.
Apply waterproofing and tanking systems to wet areas, basements, podium decks, balconies, roofs, and below-ground structures per NZBC E2, E3, and manufacturer specifications.
Install liquid-applied waterproofing membranes including polyurethane, acrylic, bituminous, and cementitious systems.
Install sheet membrane waterproofing systems including torch-on bituminous membranes, TPO, EPDM, and PVC membrane systems.
Prepare substrates for waterproofing application including grinding, patching, priming, and applying crack bridging treatments.

Adjust cabinet doors, drawer runners, hinges, and hardware to achieve smooth operation and flush alignment.
Carry out on-site modifications to cabinetry including scribing to walls, cutting out for plumbing, and fitting cornices.
Install shop joinery, reception counters, display units, and commercial fit-out joinery in retail and hospitality projects.
Read and interpret joinery shop drawings, kitchen plans, and elevation drawings accurately.
Ensure all joinery installation is square, plumb, and level, and complies with relevant NZ Standards.
Maintain all installation tools, power tools, and equipment in a clean, safe, and operational condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and use appropriate PPE at all times.
Install, commission, test, and maintain passenger lifts, goods lifts, escalators, moving walkways, and vertical platform lifts.
Install lift guide rails, car slings, counterweights, traction machines, hydraulic cylinders, and associated equipment in lift shafts.
Install lift control panels, door operators, car operating panels, hall call buttons, and safety circuits.
Commission and test lift installations including safety device testing, load testing, and performance verification.
Carry out routine maintenance, safety inspections, and statutory testing of lifts and escalators per AS 1735 requirements.
Diagnose and repair lift and escalator faults including motor, controller, safety device, and door system failures.
Carry out modernisation works on existing lifts including controller upgrades, door system replacement, and machine upgrades.
Ensure all lift works comply with AS 1735 (Lifts, Escalators and Moving Walks), NZBC, and Worksafe NZ requirements.
Liaise with building owners, facility managers, and building consent authorities for lift inspections and annual safety certificates.
Read and interpret lift installation drawings, wiring diagrams, and maintenance manuals accurately.
Maintain all lift installation tools, test equipment, and PPE in a safe and calibrated condition.
Comply with all site Health and Safety requirements, HSWA 2015 obligations, and confined space procedures for working in lift pits.
